blocks.content: Throughout his life, Shirone had never judged others based on what they possessed.
The inertia of those 18 years made the alarm of doubt ring in his mind.
Only then did he feel like his head was starting to work.
This was a trap of a false dilemma.
It was a battle where no matter what he chose, Zion would win.
The reason the logic of a false dilemma is scary is that it preemptively cuts off possibilities other than the given choices.
They present something bad and something worse, inducing you to choose the bad option to make you feel like you gained something, but in reality you are being played by the enemy.
Shirone realized that he had misunderstood what Uorin was trying to say.
No one gives away even a single gold coin without reason.
In other words, no one will help those who give up.
It was a delusion to think that if he begged with a pitiful face, Terraje Faction would spare him.
The palace is a den of predators.
Show weakness, and they'll strip the flesh from your bones.
That was what Uorin had been trying to convey.
If you want to live, growl.
Bare your teeth and growl.
Wield your claws so that no one thinks you're easy prey.
The moment you stop fighting, every beast in the royal palace will pounce on you to devour you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huuuuu!
Shirone finally exhaled.
He didn't know how long he had been holding his breath, but the air kept coming out endlessly, as if even the stray thoughts cluttering his mind were being expelled.
As his mind cleared, the target became clear.
Shirone assumed the proper stance, preparing to cast his magic.
But the nobles continued to sneer.
In their minds, if Shirone had any sense, he would have deployed a fast and flashy technique by now.
The experts can judge magical skills, and what they wanted to verify was Shirone's will.
With such a lukewarm approach, no noble would be convinced.
But Shirone didn't care.
He would become a fierce beast.
With that thought, he focused his mind, and a Photon Cannon burst into existence before him.
Shirone compressed the photons endlessly.
The ball of light contracted and expanded, trembling violently with intensity.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Not enough. This still won't quench my anger!
Shirone continued to compress the sphere of light.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Tears welled up in my eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Memories from the past three days came flooding back to him all at once.
As he recalled his mother's frightened appearance, his Photon Cannon compressed rapidly and transformed into a state of blinding white light that could no longer contain the photons.
Photon Cannon, now the size of a human head, trembled violently, as if it would burst from its center.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It was too extreme, and the unpredictability made it dangerous!
But before she could shout at Shirone, the Photon Cannon penetrated Ataraxia.
Flash!
A huge tunnel of light appeared in front of Shirone, pushing away everything in its path.
Even the atmosphere.
A deafening explosion followed, leaving everyone's eardrums ringing, while the intense light erased vision momentarily.
For a time, everything was dark.
As the eyes adapted to the scenery, the world that was revealed was completely different from a second ago.

Not only the inner walls of the Colosseum but even the distant ridges of the surrounding hills had disappeared, reduced to dust, with the particles now floating above the ground like a massive cloud of smoke.
When the wind began to disperse the dust, the path left by the Photon Cannon became clear.
A flat plain, wide enough for dozens of carriages to pass through, stretched endlessly to the horizon.

blocks.speaker: Fat Noble
blocks.content: What insolence! What is the meaning of this?!
A fat noble slammed his chair and stood up, furious.
The other nobles were just as shaken.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What would have happened if the magic had been directed toward the audience?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: If Shirone had turned in that direction, the knights would have beheaded him instantly, but that would have been within the rules.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: In fact, didn't they also let their guard down, thinking that a student was demonstrating magic?

blocks.speaker: Fat Noble
blocks.content: That, that bastard...
Zion trembled with anger.
It was not only that Shirone had chewed up his proposal like a dog chewing gum, but the terrifying power of magic had shattered his pride.
Does it even make sense for a human to push away a mountain?
No, even if it's possible, it's at the level of an archmage.
The fact that a mere aspiring mage could wield such powers spoke volumes about how unfair the human world was.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Such overwhelming power should only belong to royalty.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: If a mere bastard with no power, wealth, or status was allowed to possess such strength, how could the kingdom possibly manage to endure?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: I heard your answer clearly, Shirone. I'll be watching you!
Zion shouted loud enough for everyone to hear and stormed toward the exit.
A large group of nobles followed him out.
It was likely the Terraje Faction, but Shirone didn't care.
He was just trying to hold on to his consciousness that was reeling from the aftereffects of casting the full-power Photon Cannon.
This was the first time he hadn't collapsed after casting Ataraxia.
It was thanks to his increased endurance, but it was also a testament to how fiercely he had willed himself to stay standing.
Shirone turned his head with an intense gaze.
Uorin was still in her seat.
But her only reaction was slightly raising her eyebrows and lifting the corners of her mouth as if mocking.
Rising from her seat, she whispered something to her cat and left the Colosseum without hesitation.
Late Night Ball (1)
Orkamph's steps were quicker than usual.
He was not one to get easily excited, but this time he found it hard to control his emotions.
Only after pushing open the door to the study and gulping down the water on the desk could he calm his mind.
Ataraxia.
It was a truly catastrophic magic.
No, was this really such a new revelation?
What he saw was the power of the archangel, the supreme commander of Heaven.
And it was said that there were seven more archangels of equal strength in Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Would Valkyrie alone really be enough to oppose them?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Enough. Let's not look too far ahead. Anyway, it's a good thing for me.
If only they could replicate Ataraxia, then joining the 'Crusade', the supreme national gathering of Valkyrie, would not be a dream.
The 12 most powerful monarchs in the world.
Although the position of the 'Three Emperors', to which Empress Terraje belongs, was unreasonable, the 'Seven Kings' below that is sufficiently worth considering.
If that happens, even Terraje will have no choice but to recognize Kazra's independence.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: What would it feel like to freely handle the world's economy, culture, society, life, knowledge, and art?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Orkamph was already imagining such things.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: How is it?
As Orkamph asked, Arius appeared from a corner of the study.
Such an entrance would normally be displeasing from a monatch's perspective, but Arius had an odd stubbornness in him.
However, unlike before, today he wasn't being particularly theatrical.
His face was as flushed as Orkamph's.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: It was certainly amazing. Ataraxia, a wonderful ability.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: That's not what I'm asking. Can we replicate it now?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: It's impossible.
Orkamph thought he had misheard.
But no, his ears were fine.
Suppressing his rising anger was no easy task.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: What do you mean it can't be done? Is it another capsulization issue?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: That's one primary reason, yes. But even if we broke the capsulization, it wouldn't make a difference.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Ataraxia is a power that transcends human understanding.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: In other words, even Shirone himself doesn't fully comprehend it.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Does that make sense? I clearly saw with my own two eyes Shirone casting Ataraxia. Then are you saying Shirone is not human?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: In a sense, yes. Something non-human resides within his human body.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Shirone doesn't understand and cast Ataraxia either.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Perhaps this explanation will make more sense: it simply works.
Orkamph's eyes grew cold.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Speak clearly. You must convince me with your answer this time.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: The most likely scenario is that Ataraxia is engraved deep within Shirone's subconscious.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Perhaps it's close to instinct?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: If that's the case, it's not surprising that he can wield the power without understanding the principle.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: After all, we don't need to understand how to breathe in order to breathe.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Instinct. Then can't we extract it from there?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Well, instincts are part of what we call primal consciousness, governed at a genetic level.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: No matter how skilled a grave robber I am, diving into that depth is impossible.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Or rather, it's possible to dive in, but I would be crushed by Shirone's subconscious before I could retrieve anything.
Orkamph slammed the desk.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: All he had heard so far was that it couldn't be done.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: And now Arius was saying he wouldn't even attempt it for fear of losing his life.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: There is one way.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Orkamph changed his expression as if nothing had happened.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Of course, the Seven Sins of the Black Line wouldn't have blabbed about his incompetence without having a solution in mind.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: I apologize for not mentioning it sooner.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: But this method could be quite... difficult.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: It will require you, Your Majesty, to make a significant commitment.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I am always ready for whatever it takes.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: What is this method?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: W- What did you say?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: That, that can't be. Do you know what that means?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Of course.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: But if we use this method, we can definitely obtain Ataraxia.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Obtaining Ataraxia. Possessing an ability designated as triple S in Valkyrie.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Kazra's independence. Seven Kings. Various thoughts flashed through his mind.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: But… Is it really necessary to go that far?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Your Majesty, I've said this many times, but we don't have time.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: It's time for a quick decision.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Just give me a little, a little time to think.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Of course, this is a matter that requires time.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: I understand.
Arius paid his respects and left the study.
Walking quickly down the corridor, he leaned against the wall where no one was and spat out curses.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Tsk, stupid bastard. Pathetic fool. Indecisive shit.
Of course, Orkamph's reaction was normal.
After finding his bloodline after 18 years, he called him and made him endure all kinds of humiliation and insults, and now he's trying to take away the most precious thing.
But a king's ruthlessness should know no limits.
Judging that this was acceptable but that isn't, having such barriers to judgment means that the vessel is small.
If you start to hesitate, you can't have anything.
Even if you follow the suggestion later, the pangs of conscience will disturb your mind.
Such people will ruin everything someday.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: What a disappointment, Orkamph.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: I thought you were a cold-hearted monarch, but you're no different from a common noble.
